title: Dataset: EEG-Controlled Exoskeleton for Walking and Standing - A Longitudinal Study of Healthy Individuals
description: This longitudinal brain-machine interface dataset comprises multimodal neurophysiological and kinematic recordings from seven healthy adults performing motor imagery tasks to control a lower-limb exoskeleton. The dataset includes 60-channel EEG with EOG, dual inertial measurement units, and exoskeleton control signals across nine sessions per participant, combining open-loop calibration and closed-loop BMI control paradigms for walking and standing motor imagery.
methods_description: Data were collected from 7 healthy adults (ages 20-30 years) across 9 sessions each. Each session consisted of a motor imagery training phase for BMI decoder calibration followed by 12 closed-loop trials organized into 3 blocks, plus two 6-minute extended motor imagery tasks (walk and stop). EEG was recorded from 60 scalp channels plus 4 EOG channels. Inertial measurement units (3-axis accelerometer, gyroscope, magnetometer, and quaternion) were mounted on the participant's forehead and exoskeleton back brace. Exoskeleton control signals and BMI predictions were logged throughout.

funding_references.funder_name: UH–Methodist Graduate Fellowship for Translational Research
